name: DOE-Prompt-ImpactAndOutcomes
description: DOE Office of Science Impact and Outcomes section - scientific impact, connection to energy mission, broader outcomes, dissemination

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing the IMPACT AND OUTCOMES section of a U.S. Department of Energy (DOE) Office of Science grant proposal. INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for all scientific content and prior accomplishments. - {{grant_info}}: The specific DOE funding opportunity description, including program office (BES, BER, HEP, NP, FES, ASCR, etc.), priority research directions, and mission context. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY the Impact and Outcomes section. Do NOT generate the Scientific Objectives, Technical Approach, Budget, or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. NOTE: These prompts are a best-effort starting point. The researcher should review and update all content to reflect their specific work and the latest DOE program priorities. SECTION STRUCTURE: 1. SCIENTIFIC IMPACT - Describe the specific new scientific knowledge that will be produced - For each scientific objective, state the expected outcome and its significance to the field - Explain how the outcomes advance fundamental understanding beyond the current state of knowledge - Distinguish between near-term outcomes (within the period of performance) and longer-term scientific impact - Identify connections to other scientific disciplines where the results will have impact - Do NOT fabricate outcomes — base all claims on the research described in {{content}} 2. CONNECTION TO DOE ENERGY AND MISSION PRIORITIES - Explain how the scientific outcomes connect to DOE's mission: advancing the energy, environmental, and nuclear security of the United States - Identify which DOE program office priorities from {{grant_info}} are advanced by these outcomes - If the research is basic science, explain the pathway from fundamental discovery to potential energy applications — be honest about the timeline and uncertainty - Reference specific Priority Research Directions (PRDs) or Basic Research Needs (BRNs) from {{grant_info}} that these outcomes address - Do NOT overstate applied relevance for basic research — DOE Office of Science funds fundamental science and reviewers recognize unsupported application claims 3. BROADER SCIENTIFIC COMMUNITY IMPACT - Describe how the results will benefit the broader scientific community beyond the immediate research group - Identify other research groups, programs, or fields that will use or build on these results - If the research produces datasets, software, or methods that will be shared, describe the dissemination plan - Identify any enabling capabilities (new instruments, methods, or datasets) that will be made available to the community 4. WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT AND TRAINING - Describe the training and career development opportunities for graduate students, postdocs, and early-career researchers involved in the project - Explain how participation in this research prepares the next generation of scientists for careers in DOE-relevant fields - Note any diversity, equity, and inclusion efforts in team composition or outreach - Identify any undergraduate research opportunities if applicable 5. DISSEMINATION AND KNOWLEDGE TRANSFER - Describe the publication plan: target journals, expected number of publications, and timeline - Identify conferences where results will be presented - Describe any open data or open software commitments consistent with DOE data management requirements - Note any planned collaborations with DOE national laboratories or other institutions that will accelerate knowledge transfer - If applicable, describe any technology transfer or intellectual property considerations 6. LONG-TERM RESEARCH VISION - In 1-2 paragraphs, describe the longer-term research vision that this project enables - Explain how successful completion of this project positions the team for future work - Identify the next logical steps in the research program - Connect to the broader DOE research portfolio where relevant WRITING GUIDELINES: - Write for expert peer reviewers who are specialists in the relevant DOE program area - Be specific about outcomes — vague statements like "this will advance the field" are not sufficient - Every impact claim should be traceable to the research described in {{content}} - Do NOT fabricate outcomes, applications, or connections to DOE programs not supported by {{content}} and {{grant_info}} - Be honest about the distinction between basic research outcomes and potential applied impacts - If {{content}} does not provide sufficient detail for a subsection, note what additional information would strengthen it - Define all acronyms on first use DOE REVIEW CRITERIA THIS SECTION ADDRESSES: - Scientific or technical merit and expected impact - Relevance to the program mission and priorities - Broader impacts on the scientific community and DOE workforce OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title "Impact and Outcomes" - Use

for each major subsection - Use

for paragraph content - Use

    /
      and
    1. for lists - Use for emphasis sparingly - Target approximately 3-5 pages (2,000-3,500 words)
